title: JSONB storage, operators, and indexing
description: JSONB stores parsed binary, supports containment operators, and is indexed with gin or expression indexes.
body: Use when querying or indexing JSONB. JSONB stores a decomposed binary form (fast to read, no duplicate keys, no whitespace) and supports operators like @> (contains), ->/->> (extract), and jsonpath. A gin index accelerates containment/existence queries; jsonb_path_ops makes a smaller gin index supporting @> specifically; an expression index on a frequently-extracted scalar (e.g. ((data->>'status'))) speeds equality on that field. Do not expect a btree on the whole jsonb column to help containment. Actions: index by the actual operator used and confirm with EXPLAIN. Validation signals include the gin/expression index appearing in the plan. Risk boundary: large JSONB documents that are toasted and rewritten on every update cause write amplification and bloat.
